title: Files, Directories, Links, and Metadata · Operations Case: Create Copy Move Remove
summary: Prepare a service workspace while preserving names, metadata, link intent, and the ability to reverse every operation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux create copy move remove increment in a reversible filesystem-change plan and evidence tree. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use mkdir, touch, cp, mv, and rm with explicit targets, collision checks, and recovery-aware previews.
Identify regular files, directories, symbolic links, devices, sockets, and pipes from metadata rather than filename appearance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: linux-create-copy-move-remove — Use mkdir, touch, cp, mv, and rm with explicit targets, collision checks, and recovery-aware previews.
set -o noglob
printf 'target=%q\n' "reports/run-5.txt"
mkdir -p -- reports
touch -- "reports/run-5.txt"
stat -- "reports/run-5.txt"
